title: Deploy AI Persona with EdgeOne Pages and Blob backend

decision.rationale: Reuse the existing EdgeOne Pages Functions code for /api/submit-result and /api/export.csv with the least migration work.
decision.domain_policy: Use the default free EdgeOne Pages URL first; buy a custom domain only after the public pilot is stable.

goals: Record completed quiz results for backend statistics.
Add basic anti-spam and anti-bot protection before broader sharing.
Provide a protected CSV export for analysis.
non_goals: Do not buy a VPS for the first deployment.
Do not buy a custom domain before the pilot works on the default URL.
Do not collect names, emails, or open-text identity fields.
Do not call the response data validated survey evidence before a pilot review.
acceptance_criteria: EdgeOne Pages serves the index page and both Chinese and English quiz pages.
POST /api/submit-result returns ok:true for a valid completed quiz payload.
Results are stored in the configured Blob store under date-grouped keys.
CSV export includes at least timestamp, locale, quiz version, answer vector, scores, profile code, and diagnostics.
A basic anti-abuse layer is active before broad sharing.
Public pages still work if result recording fails.

todo.title: Audit current EdgeOne backend functions
todo.deliverable: Short note confirming current API behavior, storage key format, export auth, and missing anti-abuse controls.
todo.completion_note: Audited in planning/2026-06-09-edgeone-pages-blob-deployment.md. submit-result stores records under results/YYYY-MM-DD/YYYYMMDDhhmmssmmm-uuid.json; export.csv requires EXPORT_TOKEN or RESULTS_EXPORT_TOKEN; missing control was frontend CAPTCHA/rate-limit enforcement.

todo.title: Lock minimum response schema
todo.deliverable: Data schema note in data/README.md or a backend design note.
todo.completion_note: Minimum accepted response schema and privacy policy are documented in data/README.md.

todo.title: Add anti-abuse gate to submissions
recommended_controls: Turnstile or platform CAPTCHA token on final submission
Server-side token verification before Blob write
Request body size limit already capped at 64KB
Optional coarse rate limit by provider feature or hashed request fingerprint
todo.deliverable: Valid submissions still work; obvious bot/malformed submissions are rejected.
todo.completion_note: Strict schema validation, 64KB body limits, sensitive-field stripping, and optional server-side Turnstile verification are implemented in edge-functions/api/submit-result.js. Frontend Turnstile token capture and provider-side rate limiting remain before broad sharing.

todo.title: Configure EdgeOne project
human_actions: Create or select EdgeOne Pages project.
Confirm build settings for a static HTML project.
Create or bind Blob store named ai-ideology-results.
todo.completion_note: Created EdgeOne Pages project ai-persona, project id pages-esxansdhquzd. Set EXPORT_TOKEN in EdgeOne environment variables. Latest production deployment id dplmpe591y9m.

todo.title: Deploy static pages and API functions
todo.deliverable: Public EdgeOne URL with working quiz and backend capture.
todo.completion_note: Deployed a cleaned public bundle to https://ai-persona-qxad5fjx.edgeone.cool. Public HTML routes return 200 and match the current deployment bundle by SHA-256. The root path now serves the full current quiz page instead of the older two-card index entry. POST /api/submit-result accepted smoke payloads and /api/export.csv rejects missing token with 401 while returning CSV with a valid bearer token.

todo.title: Run end-to-end smoke test
test_paths: Confirm submit status shows success or graceful failure
Export CSV and verify both test rows
todo.deliverable: Smoke-test note with public URL, test timestamp, and known issues.
todo.completion_note: CLI/curl smoke completed on 2026-06-09: static pages 200, backend POST ok:true on deploy-token URL and naked public host, export auth works, CSV contains two smoke rows. Remaining: manual full Chinese quiz, manual full English quiz, mobile viewport check, and share-card check.

todo.title: Prepare lightweight dashboard statistics
todo.deliverable: CSV-based analysis script or simple local dashboard.

todo.title: Decide custom domain later
todo.decision_rule: Buy a custom domain only after backend capture, export, and anti-abuse are stable on the free EdgeOne URL.

open_questions.question: Should final submission require CAPTCHA for every user, or only after suspicious behavior?
open_questions.default: Require it only at final submission, not at page entry.

open_questions.question: Should provider logs be enough for abuse investigation, or should the app store a temporary hashed request fingerprint?
open_questions.default: Use provider logs only for the first pilot.

open_questions.question: Should CSV export stay token-protected only, or should it also be restricted by platform access control?
open_questions.default: Token-protected export first, platform access control if EdgeOne supports it easily.
